Exception handlers for double free and heap corruption in process management.

# Purpose
The code is part of a C library that manages heap memory for applications, specifically focusing on detecting and handling errors related to double freeing of memory and heap corruption. It includes functions to set exception handlers for these errors, which are determined based on the version of the application SDK. The [`process_heap_set_exception_handlers`](<#process_heap_set_exception_handlers>) function configures the heap to use different error handling strategies depending on the SDK version of the application. For older SDK versions, it logs warnings when a double free or heap corruption is detected. For newer SDK versions, it logs the error and then terminates the application to alert developers to the issue.

The code includes several static functions such as [`prv_warn_on_double_free`](<#prv_warn_on_double_free>), [`prv_croak_on_double_free`](<#prv_croak_on_double_free>), [`prv_warn_on_heap_corruption`](<#prv_warn_on_heap_corruption>), and [`prv_croak_on_heap_corruption`](<#prv_croak_on_heap_corruption>), which are used as handlers for these error conditions. These functions utilize logging to inform developers of the errors and, in some cases, use the `PBL_CROAK` macro to terminate the application. The code also includes logic to compare the SDK version of the application with predefined versions to decide the appropriate error handling strategy. This ensures backward compatibility while encouraging developers to address memory management issues in their applications.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `process_heap.h`
- `applib/app_logging.h`
- `process_management/pebble_process_info.h`
- `process_management/pebble_process_md.h`
- `system/passert.h`
- `util/heap.h`


# Functions

---
### prv\_warn\_on\_double\_free<!-- {{#callable:prv_warn_on_double_free}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/process_management/process_heap.c#L25>)

Logs an error message when a double free is detected on a given pointer.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ptr``: A pointer that is suspected to have been freed more than once.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Logs an error message at the error level using `APP_LOG`.
    - The log message includes the memory address of the pointer `ptr` that is suspected of being freed twice.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs logging as a side effect.


---
### prv\_croak\_on\_double\_free<!-- {{#callable:prv_croak_on_double_free}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/process_management/process_heap.c#L29>)

Logs an error and triggers a croak when a double free is detected on a pointer.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ptr``: A pointer that is suspected to have been freed more than once.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Logs an error message with the pointer address using `APP_LOG` at the error level.
    - Triggers a croak with the message indicating a double free on the pointer using `PBL_CROAK`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function logs an error and may terminate the application.


---
### prv\_warn\_on\_heap\_corruption<!-- {{#callable:prv_warn_on_heap_corruption}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/process_management/process_heap.c#L37>)

Logs an error message indicating heap corruption around a given pointer.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ptr``: A pointer to the memory location suspected of heap corruption.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Logs an error message at the error level using `APP_LOG`, indicating that there is heap corruption around the memory location pointed to by `ptr`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs logging as a side effect.


---
### prv\_croak\_on\_heap\_corruption<!-- {{#callable:prv_croak_on_heap_corruption}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/process_management/process_heap.c#L42>)

Logs an error and triggers a croak when heap corruption is detected around a given pointer.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ptr``: A pointer to the memory location where heap corruption is detected.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Logs an error message indicating heap corruption around the specified pointer using `APP_LOG`.
    - Triggers a croak with the same error message using `PBL_CROAK`, which likely causes the application to terminate or handle the error in a specific way.
- **Output**: No return value; the function logs an error and triggers a croak.


---
### process\_heap\_set\_exception\_handlers<!-- {{#callable:process_heap_set_exception_handlers}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/process_management/process_heap.c#L47>)

Sets exception handlers for double free and heap corruption based on the app's SDK version.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``heap``: A pointer to the `Heap` structure where exception handlers will be set.
    - ``app_md``: A pointer to the `PebbleProcessMd` structure containing metadata about the app, including its SDK version.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the SDK version of the app using `process_metadata_get_sdk_version(app_md)` and store it in `app_sdk_version`.
    - Compare `app_sdk_version` with `old_style_double_free_handling_version` (version 5.1).
    - If `app_sdk_version` is less than or equal to 5.1, set the double free handler to `prv_warn_on_double_free` using [`heap_set_double_free_handler`](<../../libutil/heap.c.md#heap_set_double_free_handler>).
    - If `app_sdk_version` is greater than 5.1, set the double free handler to `prv_croak_on_double_free`.
    - Compare `app_sdk_version` with `old_style_heap_corruption_version` (version 5.0x38).
    - If `app_sdk_version` is less than 5.0x38, set the heap corruption handler to `prv_warn_on_heap_corruption` using [`heap_set_corruption_handler`](<../../libutil/heap.c.md#heap_set_corruption_handler>).
    - If `app_sdk_version` is greater than or equal to 5.0x38, set the heap corruption handler to `prv_croak_on_heap_corruption`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function sets exception handlers on the provided `heap`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`version_compare`](<../../../tests/stubs/stubs_pebble_process_info.h.md#version_compare>)
    - [`heap_set_double_free_handler`](<../../libutil/heap.c.md#heap_set_double_free_handler>)
    - [`heap_set_corruption_handler`](<../../libutil/heap.c.md#heap_set_corruption_handler>)
